text: Unit testing checks each small part of an application separately, like a single function or method. Integration testing goes further, verifying that different modules work together correctly. Test-driven development, or TDD, reverses the usual order: you write the test before the actual code. This approach forces you to think carefully about what the code should do first. Together, these practices build confidence in the software. When you can run a comprehensive suite of tests quickly, you know that recent changes haven't broken anything. This safety net allows developers to refactor and improve continuously.
